# Work Order & Incident Report — DOC1170
**Document type:** Work Order / Incident Narrative (synthetic narrative record)
**Failure:** F1110 — Random Trip
**Equipment:** EQ-HBS-02 — Hot Blast Stove B (Rajendra Steel Plant, Mumbai Unit - Ironmaking)
**Event timestamp:** 2026-06-27 17:39:01  |  **RCA:** RCA1110 (closed 2026-07-01)

## 1. Incident Narrative
At 17:39:01 on 2026-06-27, the Hot Blast Stove B raised a random trip alarm and was taken out of normal operation. Condition monitoring captured air temperature 300.8 K, process temperature 310.8 K, rotational speed 1170 rpm, torque 37.1 Nm and accumulated tool wear 182 minutes at the moment of the trip. The shift crew isolated the equipment under the plant permit-to-work system and raised this work order for investigation and repair.

## 2. Response & Diagnosis
Anita Iyer (Shift A), Sunita Rao (Shift A) attended the equipment and completed the make-safe checks before handover to the investigation team. The root cause analysis RCA1110, performed by Naveen Malhotra (equipment engineer, Shift C), concluded: Hot Blast Stove B tripped in a way that did not resolve to a single deterministic cause: sensor traces were within normal bands before the event and no refractory, valve or burner defect was found. It is classified as a random failure, most consistent with a transient external disturbance during otherwise-normal useful-life operation.

## 3. Procedure Review
The investigation reviewed the governing procedure PROC-008 for this asset and recorded a compliance finding: Step 4 of PROC-008 ("Record final temperatures and any anomalies in the shift log.") — the routine monitoring entry was missing from the shift log prior to the event. This finding is the actionable link between the maintenance routine and the failure mechanism, and it was logged so recurrence checks can compare like-for-like across the fleet.

## 4. Corrective Work Completed
Reset and returned the stove to service under enhanced monitoring and scheduled a confirmatory inspection; no design change is indicated for a one-off random event. Parts and consumables used were booked to this work order, and post-repair verification confirmed the equipment returned to its qualified operating envelope before release back to production.

## 5. Closure
Work order closed on 2026-07-01 with the corrective action verified in service. This record is a synthetic narrative generated for the Synapse knowledge graph and is tagged accordingly; it mirrors the structure of plant work-order documentation for retrieval purposes. Linked records: failure F1110, RCA RCA1110, procedure PROC-008, equipment EQ-HBS-02.
